content.problemStatement:
There are NNN thieves numbered 1,2,вА¶,N1, 2, \ldots, N1,2,вА¶,N.

The thief numbered iii has a level of AiA_iAiвАЛ and has BiB_iBiвАЛ gold coins with him. All the levels AiA_iAiвАЛ are distinct integers in the range 111 to NNN. A thief iii can steal from another thief jjj if and only if his level is greater than the level of the other thief, i.e. Ai>AjA_i > A_jAiвАЛ>AjвАЛ.

A single thief can only try to steal from at most KKK other thieves since otherwise he will be caught for sure. Also, he cannot steal from the same thief twice.

For each thief, you want to know the maximum number of gold coins they can steal. The answer for each thief should be independent of the others, and you may assume, when calculating the answer for a particular thief, that other thieves won't steal from anybody and also not interfere in his plan.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and KKK - the number of thieves and boxes, and the maximum stealing parameter.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,вА¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1вАЛ,A2вАЛ,вА¶,ANвАЛ, the levels of the thieves.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,вА¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1вАЛ,B2вАЛ,вА¶,BNвАЛ, the number of gold coins each thief has.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN integers - the maximum amount each thief can steal for thieves 111, 2,вА¶,N2, \ldots, N2,вА¶,N in this order.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2вЛЕ1052 \cdot 10^52вЛЕ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : Thief numbered 111 steals from thief numbered 333 since that is the only thief he can steal from.
Thief numbered 222 can steal from both thieves 111 and 333, but he is limited to stealing from at most one person. That's why he chooses thief 333 since he has more gold coins.
Thief numbered 333 cannot steal from anybody.
